message:
fix(connector): fix escaped-quote bypass, CRDT causal semantics, registry security, and TestClock overflow

Four independent connector/testing bug fixes:

1. canonical_serializer: contains_float_marker() toggled string-tracking
   state on every '"' byte including escaped quotes like '\"', causing
   false positive/negative float detection. Count preceding backslashes
   and only toggle on unescaped quotes. Add regression tests.

2. crdt: replace naive OR-Set (plain BTreeSet<String> for adds/removes)
   with causal dot-based add/remove semantics. The old implementation
   permanently tombstoned removed elements so a concurrent add on another
   replica was silently discarded, violating the "add wins over concurrent
   remove" CRDT invariant. Each add now carries a (replica_id, counter)
   dot; remove only tombstones tags observed at remove-time. Add tests
   for concurrent add-wins-over-remove and re-add-after-remove.

3. ecosystem_registry: block mutations on revoked extensions --
   add_compatibility() and deprecate_extension() now check for Revoked
   status and return RegistryError::Revoked. Also include event_type
   in compute_audit_hash() to prevent hash collisions between entries
   differing only in event type. Add regression tests.

4. lab_runtime: TestClock::advance() called split_off(&(new_tick + 1))
   which overflows when new_tick is u64::MAX, causing timers at the
   terminal tick to never fire. Split at new_tick instead and manually
   extract callbacks at exactly new_tick from the remaining map. Add
   u64::MAX timer test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
